mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline or flattening in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months signals accommodative monetary policy, compressing the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend effect independent of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above a rising 6-month moving average, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that lead investors to price in weaker consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its trailing 6-month moving average by more than 0.1pp
failure_condition: In triggered instances, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment strength that jointly forecasts sustained economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. Neither series alone captures the compounding demand signal; the interaction of both rising together is required.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint RSAFS-HOUST momentum interaction term with forward 60d SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or single-series RSAFS or HOUST momentum alone explains equivalent predictive power (interaction term not incremental).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output and near-term earnings pressure for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week as markets react quickly to the release.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises from near-zero levels (a policy liftoff event) after an extended period below 0.25%, the initial hike introduces uncertainty about the pace of future tightening, triggering a short-term risk-off repricing of equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.20pp from a base below 0.25% (liftoff from zero lower bound)
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC scheduled to announce first rate decision since 2008 near 2015-12-16
regime_hint: monetary policy liftoff from zero lower bound
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following a liftoff event, SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of historical liftoff analogs, or no statistically significant negative drift is observed (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) alongside a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signals a turning point in the labor cycle that leads equity markets to reprice recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. Neither series alone is sufficient; the co-movement of weakening payrolls and rising unemployment together is the trigger.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth decelerates below prior 6-month average while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE increase episodes are followed by positive forward 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion phase, continued positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals durable aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. This effect is conditional on being in an expansion regime and is expected to weaken or reverse near cycle peaks.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth remains positive for at least 4 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing GDPC1 growth and forward 60d SPX return falls below 0.03 during expansion-labeled periods over any 3-year rolling window, or returns following positive GDP prints are not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production has declined for two consecutive months amid a broader manufacturing slowdown regime, the signal of an emerging supply-side recession triggers sharp short-term equity selloffs as markets reassess earnings guidance, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Following two consecutive INDPRO declines, forward 5d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or no statistically significant negative mean return is observed (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling sample.
